timeline.orderText: $~35 *IN THE HIGH COURT OF DELHI AT NEW DELHI +CS(COMM) 997/2025 LT FOODS MIDDLE EAST DMCC & ORS......Plaintiffs Through:Mr. Jai Sai Deepak, Senior Advocate with Mr. R. Abhishek & Mr. Avinash Sharma, Advocates. versus CAPITAL VENTURES PVT LTD.....Defendant Through:None. CORAM: HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE TEJAS KARIA O R D E R %01.12.2025 I.A. 29944/2025 1.This is an Application filed under Order XXXIX Rule 2A of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, read with Section 12 of the Contempt of Courts Act, 1971, for taking action against the Defendant / Contemnor for wilful disobedience and violation of the Order dated 19.09.2025. 2.The learned Senior Counsel for the Plaintiffs submits that during the course of the execution of the Local Commission on 29.09.2025, the Defendant informed the learned Local Commissioner that the Defendant has stopped using the products with the Mark ‘ROZANA’ (“Impugned Mark”) almost eight-nine months prior to 29.09.2025. The Defendant has also submitted the statement before the learned Local Commissioner showing the last use of the Impugned Mark around February-March 2025. 3.The learned Senior Counsel for the Plaintiff submits that the said statement was apparently incorrect as the Defendant is still violating the This is a digitally signed order. The authenticity of the order can be re-verified from Delhi High Court Order Portal by scanning the QR code shown above. The Order is downloaded from the DHC Server on 29/04/2026 at 14:00:07 Order dated 19.09.2025 by commercially exporting the products with the Impugned Mark as late as June 2025, which is evident from the document annexed along with this Application and which shows that the products with the Impugned Mark are available at Lulu Hyper Market in Qatar, where the distributor / agent / franchise / partner / associate / dealer of the Defendant are inviting the customers to avail the special sale. 4.The learned Senior Counsel for the Plaintiff submits that in terms of the injunction dated 19.09.2025 even export of products bearing the Impugned Mark shall amount to violation of the said order under Section 56 of the Trade Marks Act, 1999. 5.Issue Notice. Let the Notice be served upon the Defendant / Contemnor through all permissible modes. 6.Let the Reply to this Application be filed within a period of four weeks from date. Rejoinder thereto, if any, be filed before the next date of hearing. 7.It is directed that the Defendant / Contemnor shall strictly comply with the interim injunction grantedvideOrder dated 19.09.2025 and stop using, manufacturing, selling and exporting any goods or products bearing the Impugned Mark ‘ROZANA’. 8.List on 14.01.2026, the date already fixed. intelligence.summary: The court considered an application under Order XXXIX Rule 2A CPC and Section 12 of the Contempt of Courts Act for alleged violation of an interim injunction dated 19.09.2025. The plaintiff alleged that the defendant continued to export products bearing the mark 'ROZANA' after claiming to have stopped. The court issued notice to the defendant, directed compliance with the injunction, and listed the matter for 14.01.2026.

No.23448/2025 (Exemption from pre-institution Mediation) 1.This is an Application filed by the Plaintiffs seeking exemption from instituting pre-litigation Mediation under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 (‘CC Act’). 2.As the present matter contemplates urgent interim relief, in light of the judgment of the Supreme Court inYamini Manohar v. T.K.D. Krithi,2023 SCC OnLine SC 1382,exemption from the requirement of pre-institution Mediation is granted. 3.The Application stands disposed of. This is a digitally signed order. The authenticity of the order can be re-verified from Delhi High Court Order Portal by scanning the QR code shown above. The Order is downloaded from the DHC Server on 29/04/2026 at 14:00:09 CS(COMM) 997/2025Page2of17 IA No.23447/2025 (O-XI R-1(4) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908) 4.The present Application has been filed on behalf of the Plaintiffs under Order XI Rule 1(4) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (‘CPC’) as applicable to Commercial Suits under the CC Act seeking leave to place on record additional documents. 5.The Plaintiffs are permitted to file additional documents in accordance with the provisions of the CC Act and the Delhi High Court (Original Side) Rules, 2018. 6.Accordingly, the Application stands disposed of. I.A. No.23449/2025 (Exemption from advance service to the Defendant) 7.This is an Application filed by the Plaintiffs under Section 151 of the CPC seeking exemption from advance service to the Defendant. 8.Mr. Jai Sai Deepak, learned Senior Counsel for the Plaintiffs, submitted that there is a real and imminent likelihood that the Defendant may take immediate steps to dispose of, conceal or suppress its infringing business operations and digital footprints. 9.In view of the fact that the Plaintiffs have sought an urgentex-parte ad- interiminjunction along with the appointment of the Local Commissioner, the exemption from advance service to the Defendant is granted. 10.The Application is disposed of. IA No.23446/2025 11.Exemption is allowed, subject to all just exceptions. 12.The Application stands disposed of. This is a digitally signed order. Along with the Written Statement, the Defendant shall also file an Affidavit of Admission / Denial of the documents of the Plaintiffs, without which the Written Statement shall not be taken on record. 16.Liberty is granted to the Plaintiffs to file Replication, if any, within 30 days from the receipt of the Written Statement. Along with the Replication (s) filed by the Plaintiffs, an Affidavit of Admission / Denial of the documents of Defendant be filed by the Plaintiffs, without which the Replication (s) shall not be taken on record. 17.In case any Party is placing reliance on a document, which is not in their power and possession, its details and source shall be mentioned in the list of reliance, which shall also be filed with the pleadings. 18.If any of the Parties wish to seek inspection of any documents, the same shall be sought and given within the prescribed timelines. 19.List before the learned Joint Registrar on 12.11.2025 for completion of service and pleadings. I.A. No.23445/2025 (U/O XXXIX Rule 1 & 2 of CPC) 20.Issue Notice. timeline.detail: The court considered an application for contempt against Defendant No. 24 for non-compliance with a prior order. timeline.orderText: $~40 * IN THE HIGH COURT OF DELHI AT NEW DELHI + CS(COMM) 536/2025 DAZN LIMITED & ANR. .....Plaintiffs Through: Mr. Siddharth Chopra, Mr. Yatinder Garg, Mr. Akshay Matoo & Ms. Ishi Singh, Advocates Versus BUFFSPORTS. ME & ORS. .....Defendants Through: Ms. Mrinal Ojha, Mr. Debarshi Dutta, Ms. Nikita Rathi, Mr. Arjun Mookerjee & Mr. Shivam Tiwari, Advocates for D-24. CORAM: HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE TEJAS KARIA O R D E R % 12.08.2025 I.A. 19639/2025 1. This is an Application under Order XXXIX Rule 2A read with Section 151 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (“CPC”) against Defendant No. 24. The learned Counsel for the Plaintiffs submits that despite the order dated 08.07.2025, Defendant No. 24 has not complied with the same and has wilfully disobeyed the order despite repeated reminders on 08.07.2025, 11.07.2025, 12.07.2025 and 18.07.2025. 2. The learned counsel for the Defendant No. 24 submits that Defendant No. 24 is in the process of complying with the said order and the same will be complied with within a short period by taking action on the list of websites filed as Document ‘A’ containing 69 Domain Names. The learned Counsel for Defendant No. 24 submits that out of 69 Domain Names, 63 are Domains and remaining 6 are Sub-Domains. The learned Counsel for Defendant No. 24 This is a digitally signed order. The authenticity of the order can be re-verified from Delhi High Court Order Portal by scanning the QR code shown above. The Order is downloaded from the DHC Server on 29/04/2026 at 14:00:23 requests some time to comply with the said order before issuance of Notice of this Application. 3. At the request of learned Counsel for Defendant No. 24, a last and final opportunity is granted to Defendant No. 24 to comply with the order dated 08.07.2025 within a period of three days from date, failing which, this Court shall be constrained to pass appropriate directions in this Application as well as under the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021. The learned Counsel for Defendant No. 24 shall intimate compliance of the order dated 08.07.2025 to learned Counsel for the Plaintiffs within one week. 4. List on 18.08.2025. timeline.detail: The court allowed the plaintiffs' application to implead Godaddy.com LLC as defendant no.24, noting it was a necessary and proper party due to its role as domain name registrant for rogue websites. The ad-interim order dated 28.05.2025 was extended to the newly impleaded defendant. Summons and notice were issued, and a timeline for pleadings was set.
timeline.orderText: $~44 * IN THE HIGH COURT OF DELHI AT NEW DELHI + CS(COMM) 536/2025 DAZN LIMITED & ANR. .....Plaintiffs Through: Mr. Siddharth Chopra, Mr. Yatinder Garg, Mr. Akshay Maloo and Ms. Ishi Singh, Advs. versus BUFFSPORTS. ME & ORS. .....Defendants Through: Mr. Mrinal Ojha, Mr. Debarshi Dutta, Mr. Arjun Moorkerjee, Ms. Nikita Rathi, Mr. Nikhil Gupta and Mr. Yogesh Singh, Advs. for D-24/ Godaddy. CORAM: HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SAURABH BANERJEE O R D E R % 08.07.2025 I.A. 15641/2025-By plaintiffs U/O 1 R 10 seeking impleadment of GODADDY.COM LLC as domain name Registrant of the additional identified rogue domains engaged in infringing activities and extension of ad-interim order dated 28.05.2025 1. Since by virtue of the present application, the plaintiffs seek impleadment of DNR Godaddy.com LLC as the defendant no.24, learned counsel appearing for the proposed defendant no.24, at the outset, submits though there are reservations/ objections about the averments made in the present suit, however, in all fairness submits that since the defendant no.24 may be a concerned party. 2. In view of the aforesaid, learned counsel for the plaintiffs, without This is a digitally signed order. The authenticity of the order can be re-verified from Delhi High Court Order Portal by scanning the QR code shown above. The Order is downloaded from the DHC Server on 29/04/2026 at 14:00:23 going into the merits of the present application, seeks leave to withdraw the present application and orally requests this Court for impleading the said proposed defendant i.e. DNR Godaddy.com LLC as defendant no.24 in the present suit as it has not been impleaded due to sheer inadvertence. 3. Considering the nature of the proceedings, and the nature of disputes involved it is deemed necessary for impleading the proposed defendant no.24 as it is a proper party herein. Furthermore, since today numerous websites, which may be as many as more than fifty (50), are still (wrongly) operating despite the specific order dated 28.05.2025 passed by this Court in which the DNR is Godaddy.com LLC, this Court is of the view that the proposed defendant no.24 is a necessary and proper party in the present suit, which due to share inadvertence, was not impleaded as a party at the initial stage. In any event, the present proceedings are at the initial stages when the impleadment of the proposed defendant herein will not cause any impediment in the way(s) of any of the defendants herein. 4. In view of the aforesaid, DNR Godaddy.com LLC is impleaded as defendant no.24 in the present proceedings. As such, the amended memo of parties filed along with the present application is taken on record. 5. Accordingly, the order passed on 28.05.2025 shall stand extended to the defendant no.24. 6. In view of the aforesaid directions, the present application stands disposed of. CS(COMM) 536/2025 & I.A. 13756/2025-Stay 7. Issue summons of the suit and notice to the stay application to the This is a digitally signed order. The written statement/ reply be filed by the defendant no.24 within thirty days. Written statement be filed by the said defendant along with affidavit of admission/ denial of documents of the plaintiffs, without which the written statement shall not be taken on record. 10. Replication/ reply thereto, if any, be filed by the plaintiffs fifteen days from the date of receipt of written statement/ reply. The said replication, if any, shall be accompanied by with affidavit of admission/ denial of documents filed by the said defendant, without which the replication shall not be taken on record within the aforesaid period of fifteen days. 11. If any of the parties wish to seek inspection of any document(s), the same shall be sought and given within the requisite timelines. 12. List before Court on 28.08.2025. SAURABH BANERJEE, J. timeline.detail: The Joint Registrar issued notice on the plaintiffs' application for impleadment of GODADDY.COM LLC and extension of ad-interim order. After hearing arguments on the Registrar's power to extend the injunction, the matter was placed before the Hon'ble Court on 08.07.2025 at the joint request of the parties.
timeline.orderText: $~36 * IN THE HIGH COURT OF DELHI AT NEW DELHI + CS(COMM) 536/2025 DAZN LIMITED & ANR. .....Plaintiff Through: Mr. Akshay Maloo & Ms. Ishi Singh, Advs. versus BUFFSPORTS. ME & ORS. .....Defendant Through: Mr. Mrinal Ojha, Mr. Debarshi Dutta, Ms. Nikita Rathi, Advs. for D-24. CORAM: JOINT REGISTRAR (JUDICIAL), SUDHIR KUMAR SIROHI (DHJS) O R D E R % 07.07.2025 List the matter for the purpose fixed on the date already fixed i.e. 28.08.2025. IA No. 15641/2025 (By plaintiffs u/o 1 Rule 10 CPC seeking impleadment of GODADDY.COM LLC as domain name registrar of the additional identified rogue domains engaged in infringing activities and extension of ad-interim order dated 28.05.2025) Notice issued. Accepted. It has been submitted by the ld. counsel for the plaintiff that the FIFA World Cup is till 14.07.2025 and any delay in passing order on the application will further prejudice the right of the plaintiff and the order dated 28.05.2025 may be extended to the proposed defendant GODADDY.COM, on the other hand, ld. counsel for the defendant GODADDY.COM This is a digitally signed order. The authenticity of the order can be re-verified from Delhi High Court Order Portal by scanning the QR code shown above. The Order is downloaded from the DHC Server on 29/04/2026 at 14:00:23 argued that the Court of Joint Registrar does not have any power to extend the order unless specifically mentioned in the order of the Hon'ble Court. The Court of Joint Registrar has power to deal with the application u/o 1 Rule 10 but the injunction order of the Hon'ble Court cannot be extended without any express order of the Hon'ble Court and in the order dated 28.05.2025, the Hon'ble Court of Joint Registrar has not been given any power to extend the order, however the plaintiff was having remedy as per para no. 24 of the said order. At the joint request of the parties, the application is placed before the Hon'ble Court on 08.07.2025. timeline.detail: The court disposed of several applications: exempted plaintiff from pre-institution mediation, granted exemption from advance service on certain defendants, allowed filing of additional documents later, and issued summons to defendants. timeline.orderText: CS(COMM) 536/2025 Page 1 of 10 $~49 * IN THE HIGH COURT OF DELHI AT NEW DELHI + CS(COMM) 536/2025 DAZN LIMITED & ANR. .....Plaintiffs Through: Mr. Siddharth Chopra, Mr. Yatinder Garg, Mr. Akshay Maloo and Ms. Ishi Singh, Advocates. versus BUFFSPORTS. ME & ORS. .....Defendant Through: None. CORAM: HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SAURABH BANERJEE O R D E R % 28.05.2025 I.A. 13759/2025-Exemption from pre institution mediation 1. Vide the present application under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, read with Section 151 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (CPC), the plaintiff seeks exemption from pre-litigation mediation. 2. Considering the averments made in the present application, as also since the plaintiffs are seeking ex parte ad interim injunction in an accompanying application, and in view of the judgment passed by the Hon’ble Supreme Court in Yamini Manohar v. T.K.D. Krithi 2024 (5) SCC 815, which has been followed by a Division Bench this Court in Chandra Kishore Chaurasia v. R. A. Perfumery Works Private Limited 2022:DHC:4454-DB, the plaintiffs are exempted from instituting pre- litigation mediation. This is a digitally signed order. Vide the present application under Order XI Rule 1(4) read with Section 151 of the CPC, the plaintiff seeks leave of this Court to file additional documents. 2. The plaintiffs are at liberty to file additional documents at a later stage, albeit, after initiating appropriate steps, strictly as per the provisions of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 read with Section 151 of the CPC and the Delhi High Court (Original Side) Rules, 2018. 3. Accordingly, the present application stands disposed of. I.A. 13757/2025-Exp the plaintiff from issuing notice to D-13, 16, 21 and 22 under Section 80 of CPC. 1. For the reasons stated in the application as also taking into account the aforesaid factors and in the interest of justice, the plaintiff is granted exemption from effecting advance service upon defendants No. 13, 16, 21 and 22 3. Accordingly, the present application is allowed and disposed of. CS(COMM) 535/2025 4. Vide the present plaint, the plaintiff seeks grant of a permanent injunction restraining the defendants (rouge websites) engaged in infringing the plaintiff’s exclusive rights and broadcasting rights, rendition of accounts, damages, etc. 5. The summons shall state that the written statement(s) be filed by the defendant within a period of thirty days from the date of the receipt of the summons. Written statement(s) be filed by the defendant along with affidavit of admission/ denial of documents of the plaintiff, without which the written statement shall not be taken on record. 8. Replication thereto, if any, be filed by the plaintiff within a period of fifteen days from the date of receipt of written statement(s). If any of the parties wish to seek inspection of any document, the same shall be sought and given within the requisite timelines. 10. timeline.detail: The court allowed the plaintiff's application to implead additional rogue websites (defendant nos. 47 to 449) for infringing copyrighted content. The newly added defendants are subject to existing interim orders. Summons to be issued via email only.
timeline.orderText: $~22 * IN THE HIGH COURT OF DELHI AT NEW DELHI + CS(COMM) 977/2025 JIOSTAR INDIA PRIVATE LIMITED .....Plaintiff Through: None. versus VEGAMOVIES.YACHTS & ORS. .....Defendants Through: Mr. Jagdish Chandra, CGSC, Mr. Sujeet Kumar & Mr. Sankalp Mishra, Advs. for R/UOI Ms. Harshita Rathod, Adv. for D-31 & 34 CORAM: JOINT REGISTRAR (JUDICIAL), SUDHIR KUMAR SIROHI (DHJS) O R D E R % 13.02.2026 List the matter for the purpose fixed on the date fixed i.e. 27.02.2026. IA No. 4165/2026 (By plaintiff u/O I Rule 10 CPC seeking impleadment of additional rogue websites (notified on affidavits) & their domain name registrars engaged in infringing activities as defendants in the instant suit) The plaintiff is seeking to implead additional rogue websites & their domain name registrars as the defendant nos. 47 to 449 since they are illegally distributing the copyrighted content of the plaintiff. The relevant details of these infringers are mentioned in para no.6 of the application. Keeping in view, the contents of this application so read in the light of the prayers made in the suit, the same is allowed. This is a digitally signed order. The authenticity of the order can be re-verified from Delhi High Court Order Portal by scanning the QR code shown above. The Order is downloaded from the DHC Server on 03/05/2026 at 09:33:25 It is worthwhile to note that the additional rogue websites & their domain name registrars impleaded as the defendant nos. 47 to 449 shall be subject to the interim orders already passed in the suit. The amended memo of parties be taken on record. Summons be issued to the defendant nos. 47 to 449 through all permissible modes, subject to the filing of PF. At this stage, ld. counsel for the plaintiff requests to issue summons through email only as the newly identified defendants are rogue websites, therefore the summons be issued via emails, subject to the filing of PF. CORAM: HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE TEJAS KARIA O R D E R % 12.09.2025 I.A. 22642/2025 (O-XI R-1(4) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908) 1. The present Application has been filed on behalf of the Plaintiff under Order XI Rule 1(4) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 (“CPC”) as applicable to Commercial Suits under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 (“CC Act”) seeking leave to place on record additional documents. 2. The Plaintiff is permitted to file additional documents in accordance with the provisions of the CC Act and the Delhi High Court (Original Side) Rules, 2018. 3. Accordingly, the Application stands disposed of. I.A. 22643/2025 (Extension of time to file Court Fees) 4. The present Application has been filed by the Plaintiff under Section This is a digitally signed order. Considering the submissions made in the present Application, time of two weeks is granted to file the Court Fees. 6. The Application stands disposed of. I.A. 22640/2025 (Exemption from pre-institution Mediation) 7. This is an Application filed by the Plaintiff seeking exemption from instituting pre-litigation Mediation under Section 12A of the CC Act. 8. As the present matter contemplates urgent interim relief, in light of the judgment of the Supreme Court in Yamini Manohar v. T.K.D. Krithi, 2023 SCC OnLine SC 1382, exemption from the requirement of pre-institution Mediation is granted. 9. The Application stands disposed of. I.A. 22641/2025 (for exemption from giving written notice to the government authority) 10. This is an Application filed by the Plaintiff under Section 80 read with Section 151 of the CPC seeking exemption from serving two months’ prior notice under Section 80 of the CPC to Defendant No. 36, i.e., Bharat Sanchar Nigam Ltd., Defendant No. 39, i.e., Mahanagar Telephone Nigam Ltd., Defendant No. 44, i.e., Department of Telecommunications and Defendant No. 45, i.e., the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology. 11. For the reasons stated in the Application, the same is allowed. 12. The Application stands disposed of. CS(COMM) 977/2025 13. Let the Plaint be registered as a Suit. 14. Issue Summons. The learned Counsel for Defendant Nos. 31 and 34 This is a digitally signed order. The authenticity of the order can be re-verified from Delhi High Court Order Portal by scanning the QR code shown above. Let the Summons be served to the remaining Defendants through all permissible modes upon filing of the Process Fee. 16. The Summons shall state that the Written Statement(s) shall be filed by the Defendants within 30 days from the date of the receipt of Summons. Along with the Written Statement(s), the Defendants shall also file an Affidavit of Admission / Denial of the documents of the Plaintiff, without which the Written Statement(s) shall not be taken on record. 17. Liberty is granted to the Plaintiff to file Replication(s), if any, within 30 days from the receipt of the Written Statement(s). Along with the Replication(s) filed by the Plaintiff, an Affidavit of Admission / Denial of the documents of Defendants be filed by the Plaintiff, without which the Replication(s) shall not be taken on record. 18. In case any Party is placing reliance on a document, which is not in their power and possession, its details and source shall be mentioned in the list of reliance, which shall also be filed with the pleadings. 19. If any of the Parties wish to seek inspection of any documents, the same shall be sought and given within the prescribed timelines. 20. List before the learned Joint Registrar on 07.11.2025 for completion of service and pleadings. I.A. 22639/2025 (U/O XXXIX Rule 1 & 2 of CPC) 21. Issue Notice. The learned Counsel for Defendant Nos. 31 and 34 accepts Notice. 22. Notice to the remaining Defendants be served through all permissible modes upon filing of the Process Fees. 23. The present Suit has been filed for permanent injunction restraining This is a digitally signed order. The Order is downloaded from the DHC Server on 03/05/2026 at 09:33:26 CS(COMM) 977/2025 Page 4 of 13 Defendant Nos. 1 to 24 (“Websites”) and other unknown Parties from hosting, streaming, communicating in any manner and / or making available for viewing and downloading, without authorization, on the Websites or other platforms, the content of the upcoming film ‘Jolly LLB 3’ (“Film”). 24. The learned Counsel for the Plaintiff submitted that the Plaintiff is a leading media house and film production and distribution company in India, which has produced and / or acquired the exclusive media rights in various films across varied genres and languages. 25. The learned Counsel for the Plaintiff submitted that the Plaintiff commissioned Kangra Talkies Private Limited (“Kangra Talkies”) for the development and line production of the Film. Kangra Talkies vide its letter dated 21.08.2025, confirmed that the Plaintiff is the sole and exclusive owner of all the rights including all the Intellectual Property Rights and Exploitation Rights of the Film and its script, inter alia the rights for initiating or defending

timeline.orderText: $~24 * IN THE HIGH COURT OF DELHI AT NEW DELHI + CS(COMM) 1210/2025 JIOSTAR INDIA PRIVATE LIMITED .....Plaintiff Through: Mr. Priyansh Kohli, Mr. Manish Singh (VC), Advocates versus TIMZ1CW.2HSGWROTENFY7RHYME.SHOP & ORS. .....Defendant Through: Mr. Mukul Singh CGSC, Mr. Adhiraj Singh, GP for D-22 & 23 CORAM: JOINT REGISTRAR (JUDICIAL) Dr. AJAY GULATI O R D E R % 23.12.2025 I.A. 32703/2025 on behalf of the plaintiffs under Order I Rule 10 CPC seeking impleadment of additional mirrors, redirects, or alphanumeric variations and concerned DNRs as defendant nos. 25 to 66. 1. The plaintiff is seeking to implead rogue websites / alphanumeric variations as defendant nos. 25 to 60 because they have been found to be indulging in illegally disseminating the copyrighted material belonging to the plaintiff. Further, proposed defendant nos. 61 to 66 are the DNR’s for some of the proposed defendant nos. 25 to 60 whose presence is necessary in the array. Keeping in view the contents of the suit and the application, the same is allowed. The proposed defendants are impleaded as defendant nos. 25 to 66. 2. The amended memo filed alongwith this IA is taken on record. Steps be taken to serve the newly impleaded defendants This is a digitally signed order. The authenticity of the order can be re-verified from Delhi High Court Order Portal by scanning the QR code shown above. The Order is downloaded from the DHC Server on 18/05/2026 at 10:34:09 with summons within two weeks, through the electronic mode only. 3. The lock and suspension / blocking order passed by the Hon’ble Court on 13.11.2025 shall operate qua the freshly impleaded defendants as well which stand injuncted, in terms of directions contained in para 33 (a) of the order dated 13.11.2025. Further, in terms of the directions given to the DNR’s in para 33 (b) of the order dated 13.11.2025, the concerned DNR’s which are already arrayed and the newly impleaded DNR’s shall lock and suspend the domain name registrations of defendant nos. 25 to 60, and the DNR’s shall also furnish the relevant information as directed in para 33 (c) of the order dated 13.11.2025. Similarly, in terms of the directions contained in para 33 (d) and 33 (c) of the order dated 13.11.2025, the ISP’s and DoT & MEITY respectively shall comply in respect to the newly impleaded defendants / rogue websites. IA is allowed and disposed off accordingly. Dr. AJAY GULATI (DHJS), JOINT REGISTRAR (JUDICIAL) DECEMBER 23, 2025/sk This is a digitally signed order. JUSTICE MANMEET PRITAM SINGH ARORA O R D E R % 13.11.2025 I.A. 28088/2025 (seeking leave to file additional documents) 1. This is an application seeking leave to file additional documents under Order XI Rule 1(4) of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 [‘CPC’] [as amended by the Commercial Courts Act, 2015] read with Section 151 CPC. 2. The Plaintiff, if it wishes to file additional documents will file the same within thirty (30) days from today, and it shall do so strictly as per the provisions of the Commercial Courts Act and the Delhi High Court (Original Side) Rules, 2018 (‘DHC Rules’). 3. For the reasons stated in the application, the same is allowed. 4. Accordingly, the application is disposed of. This is a digitally signed order. The authenticity of the order can be re-verified from Delhi High Court Order Portal by scanning the QR code shown above. The Order is downloaded from the DHC Server on 18/05/2026 at 10:34:09 CS(COMM) 1210/2025 Page 2 of 14 I.A. 28087/2025 (seeking exemption from serving notice) 5. This is an application seeking exemption from serving notice under Section 80 CPC vis-à-vis Defendant Nos.14, 17, 22 and 23. 6. Ms. Nidhi Raman, Advocate appeared on advance notice for Defendant Nos. 22 and 23. 7. In view of the fact that Defendant Nos. 14, 17, 22 and 23 are proforma Defendants, exemption sought is granted. 8. Accordingly, the application is disposed of. I.A. 28086/2025 (seeking exemption from pre-litigation mediation) 9. This is an application under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 read with Section 151 CPC, filed by the Plaintiff seeking exemption from instituting pre-litigation mediation. 10. Having regard to the facts that the present suit contemplates urgent interim relief and in light of the judgement of the Supreme Court in Yamini Manohar v. T.K.D. Keerthi 1 , exemption from the requirement of pre- institution mediation is granted to the plaintiff. 11. Accordingly, the application stands disposed of. CS(OMM) 1210/2025 12. The present suit has been filed by the Plaintiff seeking permanent injunction restraining infringement of its copyright and broadcast reproduction rights, along with other ancillary reliefs. 13. Let the plaint be registered as a suit. 14. Issue summons to Defendant Nos.1 to 7 through e-mode. 15. The summons shall indicate that the written statements must be filed 1 (2024) 5 SCC 815 This is a digitally signed order. The Defendants shall also file affidavits of admission/denial of the documents filed by the Plaintiff, failing which the written statements shall not be taken on record. 16. The Plaintiff is at liberty to file replications thereto within thirty (30) days after filing of the written statements. The replications shall be accompanied by affidavits of admission/denial in respect of the documents filed by the Defendants, failing which the replications shall not be taken on record. 17. It is made clear that any unjustified denial of documents may lead to an order of costs against the concerned party. 18. Any party seeking inspection of documents may do so in accordance with the Delhi High Court (Original Side) Rules, 2018. 19. No summons are being issued to Defendant Nos. 8 to 23 since these are proforma Defendants. 20. Defendant No. 24 is John Doe, as Plaintiff has sought relief against unknown entities. After the Plaintiff has received Basic Subscriber Information[‘BSI’] details from the proforma Defendants with respect to the unknown entities, it shall file an amended memo of parties within two (2) weeks from receipt of an information and thereafter summons will be issued to the said identified entities. 21. List before the learned Joint Registrar (J) for completion of service and pleadings, marking of exhibits and admission/denial of documents on 02.12.2025. 22. List before the Court on 03.02.2026. This is an application filed under Order XXXIX Rule 1 and 2 CPC seeking grant of ad-interim injunction against Defendant Nos. 1 to 7. 24. Mr. Sidharth Chopra, learned counsel for the Plaintiff sets up the Plaintiff’s case as under:- 24.1 The Plaintiff is an Indian entertainment and media company engaged in producing and broadcasting content across more than 100 television channels in multiple languages including channels like Star Sports 1, Star Sports 2, Star Sports HD 1, Star Sports Select 1, Star Sports 2 HD, Star Sports Select HD 1, Star Sports Khel, Star Movies, Colors Infinity, Star Bharat, Star Gold, Jalsha Movies, Maa Movies, Star Plus, Colors, Colors Rishtey, MTV, Disney Channel, National Geographic and The History Channel to name a few [‘JioStar Channels’]. 24.2 It holds exclusive uplink and downlink rights from the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting, allowing it to broadcast major sporting events such as BCCI, ICC, and CA tournaments, and has acquired significant viewership nationwide. Service-affidavit has been filed by the plaintiff and as per same, the defendant no. 1 to 4 (rogue websites), 5 to 7 (DNR), 18 to 16 (ISPs/TSPs), 17 & 18 (DOTY & MEITY) were served on 16.12.2025 through emails. IA No. 4886/2026 (By plaintiff u/O I Rule 10 seeking impleadment of additional applications/associated domains/URLs/UIs & their domain name registrars engaged in infringing activities as the original defendants) The plaintiff is seeking to implead additional rogue websites/DNRs as the defendant nos. 22 to 31 since they are illegally distributing the copyrighted content of the plaintiff. The relevant details of this infringer are mentioned in para no.6 of the application. Keeping in view, the contents of this application so read in the light of the prayers made in the suit and in view of para no. 7 of the order of the Hon'ble Court dated 11.11.2025, the same is allowed. It is worthwhile to note that the additional rogue websites/DNRs impleaded as the defendant nos. 22 to 31 shall be subject to the interim orders already passed in the suit. The amended memo of parties be taken on record. Summons be issued to the defendant nos. 22 to 31 through all permissible modes, subject to the filing of PF. At this stage, ld. counsel for the plaintiff requests to issue summons through email only as the newly identified defendants are rogue websites, therefore the summons be issued via emails, subject to the filing of PF. JUSTICE TEJAS KARIA O R D E R %11.11.2025 I.A. 27872/2025 (Exemption from pre-institution Mediation) 1.This is an Application filed by the Plaintiff seeking exemption from instituting pre-litigation Mediation under Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015 (“CC Act”). 2.As the present matter contemplates urgent interim relief, in light of the judgment of the Supreme Court inYamini Manohar v. T.K.D. Krithi, 2023 SCC OnLine SC 1382,exemption from the requirement of pre-institution Mediation is granted. 3.The Application stands disposed of. No. 20487/2025 on behalf of the plaintiffs seeking to change the name of plaintiff no. 1. 1. The present application has been moved since plaintiff no. 1 has been converted into a Public Limited Company. Ld. Counsel for defendant no. 2 accepts notice and submits that she has no objection to the application being allowed since the application is formal in nature. 2. For the reasons stated in the application, application is allowed. Name of plaintiff no. 1 is allowed to be changed as per the amended memo of parties. IA stands disposed off. Amended memo of parties is already on record. Registry is directed to henceforth mention the title of the suit as per the amended memo of parties. timeline.detail: Counsel for defendant no.2 submitted that the impugned marks were transferred to Future Lifestyle Fashions Ltd. under a scheme of arrangement, and that company is undergoing CIRP with an IRP appointed. Plaintiff's counsel stated they would take necessary steps. The court listed the matter for further proceedings before the Joint Registrar and for final hearing.
timeline.orderText: CS(COMM) 149/2025Page1of2 $~27 *IN THE HIGH COURT OF DELHI AT NEW DELHI +CS(COMM) 149/2025 CENTRAL RETAIL CORPORATION LTD. & ANR.....Plaintiffs Through:Ms. Vaishali R. Mittal, Mr. Siddhant Chamola, Mr. Shivang Sharma and Ms. Pallavi Bhatnagar, Advocates versus FUTURE BRANDS LTD & ANR A+.....Defendant Through:Mr. Rishi Agrawala and Ms. Aarushi Tiku, Advocates CORAM: HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE AMIT BANSAL O R D E R %29.04.2025 1.Ms. Aarushi Tiku, counsel appearing for defendant no.2, submits that in terms of the scheme of arrangement sanctioned by the order passed by the Bombay High Court on 10 th May, 2013, the defendant no.2 has transferred the impugned marks in favour of ‘Future Lifestyle Fashions Ltd.’. 2.Counsel further submits that the aforesaid company is undergoing Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (‘CIRP’) and an Interim Resolution Professional (‘IRP’) has been appointedvideorder dated 04 th May, 2023, passed by the National Company Law Tribunal, Mumbai Branch. 2.1.A copy of the said order has been handed over to Ms. Vaishali Mittal, counsel appearing on behalf of the plaintiff. This is a digitally signed order. The authenticity of the order can be re-verified from Delhi High Court Order Portal by scanning the QR code shown above. The Order is downloaded from the DHC Server on 18/05/2026 at 10:34:32 CS(COMM) 149/2025Page2of2 3.Ms. Vaishali R. Mittal submits that based on the aforesaid position, the plaintiff will take the necessary steps. 4.List before the Joint Registrar on 30 th July, 2025. 5.List for final hearing on 06 th October, 2025. AMIT BANSAL, J APRIL 29, 2025 ds This is a digitally signed order. The authenticity of the order can be re-verified from Delhi High Court Order Portal by scanning the QR code shown above. The Order is downloaded from the DHC Server on 18/05/2026 at 10:34:32
